reviews.review_description:
It is not really a “game” in the traditional sense—it’s closer to an interactive training tool.

The biggest strength is how approachable it is. You’re guided step-by-step through CPR procedures, making it useful even if you’ve never had training before.

You learn when to start CPR, how to position your hand and the basic AED usage.

 It gives a mental model of what to do in an emergency, which is arguably the most important first step.

Unlucky, I didn’t had a doll to practice. So, no chest resistance or no real sense of compression depth.

This means that this app should never replace real training.

developer_response.body:
Hej POWERGIRL.BE !

Thanks a lot for your review. It means a lot to us. We're glad that you found it useful, and can see a good potential in the VR learning experience. 

We agree that CPR training with CPR Simulator does not replace physical training with a real doll, where you can feel the resistance. In that sense, CPR Simulator serves the purpose of giving a mental model of what to do in an emergency, as you write.

Thanks again for the review, and have a great day!

reviews.review_description:
Just get it! This could be a fun class activity as a commentary on a scenario  "whats good and whats bad" for group discussion. This could also be -great for layman's demo- with a few small tweaks to keep people from endangering themselves or the victim.
1) Always- the first thing you do is assess the scene for danger, do not run in yelling. This is what makes two victims on a scene.  Look up down all around!
2) Communication with bystanders. They can call EMS, get the AED, get info or keep the scene safe for you etc. We teach to recruit bystanders so a less static one would help.
 3)In assessing LOC: shaking their shoulders from on top of the person is a bad plan for safety. 
4)After opening the airway, checking for breath, activating ems, getting AED *great sequence*...We could get out our pocket/keychain PPE
5) Landmarking before compressions is deadly important. For a layman's game: drag a snapped hand from the armpit to middle before each 2 hand set up. To make this safer & more educational.
6) Compressions WORK if you use a pillow etc BUT the victim avatar loads differently around the scene & you have to shuffle your prop. Easy fix. Needs a verbal cue for straight arms, hinging up and down from your hips. 
7)Show a Pocket filter/PPE+ save more lives: It's dangerous to put your mouth on a strange mouth, you could live the rest of your life with that person's disease. If a common $1 pocket plastic barrier filter sheet was added, this would be -so good- without taking out the important breath training.
8) No "forceful" breaths, this is how we cause distension, & complications that may prevent us form saving a life. Normal breaths & we watch the chest fall in-between, easy fix.
9) Recovery position once the victim begins breathing normally, to keep airway maintained.
Looking forward to updates! Wish I could help! This game has so much love in it & clearly exists to help,  like first responders & trainers. I have mad respect for this as an evolving WIP, thank you!

developer_response.body: Hej @Kayitforward! Thank you very much for this thorough review. All your feedback points are valid, and we have started a process of backlogging these points, so that we can make sure to have them fixed/integrated in the coming updates. We are also in the process of reviewing your points with our collaborations partners PARAT and First-8, that have helped us with testing, and making sure that the training scenario lives up to the existing guidelines. I believe we are already in contact by mail, and I look forward to collaborating with you on the new updates we are releasing. Thank you again for sharing this with us, and showing that you care! Have a great day.

reviews.review_description:
Good morning.

I am a firefighter in Pataias - Portugal, a trainer in the area of ​​basic life support, and I am trying out your program in training new firefighters and the community in general, and I am very pleased with the performance demonstrated in the program.

There are indeed some changes that should be more visible because of the protocol, but in the matter of mouth-to-mouth ventilation, I think it should not be removed because it is not indicated for strangers but for close family members, and everyone has to learn how to do it.

Excellent application, I just regret that the free version does not have a scenario without a person there to give guidance, it is completely free.

Thank you for making it available to us.

Sincerely

Márcio Cristo

developer_response.body:
Hej @Marcio_Christo !

Thank you very much for your review. It means a lot to us. We're very glad you are pleased with CPR Simulator as a simulation training tool.

Regarding the mouth-to-mouth interaction we agree that the interaction/method should not be completely removed from CPR Simulator. Mostly because it's a method that all people should have the chance to practice. That said we are planning to make the choice of having 'mouth-to-mouth' on or off more visible (right now it's under 'Customise scenario' settings). A little bit like the choice of turning on or off the censoring of the woman's breast. We are also planning to add the option of using a 'pocket mask', so people can learn how to use it, and are encouraged to carry one with them in real life.

In the 'Customise scenario' settings it should be possible to turn off the instructor. If you do that the green hand indicators will also disappear. Maybe that is something you can use.

Thank you again for your great review.

Have a superb day!

reviews.review_title: Review from a layman and an ER Doctor
reviews.review_description:
As someone with no medical training, I found this app very informative. It helped me live through a scenario that I haven't been in and experience (with instruction) what I should be doing.
It's rather short (around 5 minutes of content currently) but the knowledge is useful and I can share it with other people too.

I showed this to an ER doctor and he liked it a lot as a way to expose laypeople to scenarios like this, which can only help calm them under the pressure, but was disappointed in the outdated guidance (for instance, we no longer give mouth to mouth in these cases, and we don't shave their chests). Additionally, he said that practicing chest compressions in the air (where nothing is pushing back) isn't great, and if the app recommended putting pillows or something down first it would make the experience better. 
That being said, he feels that if this was updated with the newest guidelines, and added more scenarios, this would be a great experience both for laypeople, and even for training Residents.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review, @Nameles36 ! We're very glad that you enjoyed CPR Simulator and see a big learning potential in it. Also really great that you showed it to an ER doctor. Of course we're sad to hear that the doctor was disappointed, especially when it is already possible to toggle 'mouth-to-mouth' on and off in the scenario settings. We've made it possible to have it on and off, because it varies what the guidelines say around the world. But maybe we should make that toggle even more visible, so that it's more clear to people that they have that choice. I've written that in our backlog. 

We have also noted that we could write that people could place pillows or other soft objects, that could simulate the push-back. 

Thank you for the great feedback. We look forward to releasing the new version of the scenario before summer, where it will be possible to practice CPR on a child.

Have a great day!

reviews.review_description:
I had not had experience with the defibrillator model so it was excellent to hear its example. Everything else is great for training.
Only a single scenario included, but great for practice.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review, @sparkydog ! We're glad that you had a great experience with CPR Simulator. We hope to add more scenarios/environments soon so that you can train CPR in other settings. 

Have a great day!

reviews.review_description: Knowing how to perform CPR is invaluable information, and even if you know it, getting a refresher course every now and then is equally valuable. This app does that through VR, and it is probably the second best way of learning this stuff after having a real-life instructor. Highly recommend.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review, @Cim ! It means a lot to us, and I will extend your compliments to our developers. Have a great day!

reviews.review_description: Used to be cpr certified so this was a good refresher.  One thing to point out is that this does a better job simulating a real human experience instead of using a dummy.  As a result on included step for for the AED is to cut through the shirt as the pads need to make direct skin contact.  Hand tracking isn’t a current feature but would make this feel more natural although there are limitations in how well that feature works.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review, @eagleloc ! It means a lot to us, and we're very glad that you had a good experience with CPR Simulator. We agree that hand tracking could make it feel more natural, and we're still planning to integrate that option in the future. Have a great day!

reviews.review_title: CPR in VR is a must have!
reviews.review_description:
This is the 2nd iteration of CPR Simulator I've tried. Its great to see all the enhancements and improvements made over the last year. Honestly, this is an essential app for all VR users and enthusiasts. These brief training experiences can mean life and death for a stranger or loved one. You get to learn the basics of assessing and responding to a sudden cardiac event in the comfort of your own home and VR headset. 
Check it out. It could be the difference between life and death!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the encouraging words, Rick! This means a lot to us. We're on a journey to democratise quality life saving simulation training, and your review will definitely help us on that journey. Thanks again!

reviews.review_description:
If you're like me, who always brings your headset over to family gatherings and so on, this is one of the must-have apps.

With it, I'm able to teach and prepare them in case something bad happens (God forbid).

Also, I think it would be a great addition to also teach other techniques, like what to do when someone is choking.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review, @CaioOliveiraW! We got really excited while reading your review. It's exactly what we hope people will do, when having access to CPR Simulator: Share the learning experience with their family and friends. It's a quite wild coincidence that you mention, that you would like the ability to train what to do in a choking scenario. It's exactly what we are developing these days, and hope to be done with in June 2024. People who have access to CPR Simulator will also have access to the 'Heimlich Maneuver' scenario, and vice versa. We hope you will enjoy that learning scenario as well. Have a great day.

reviews.review_description: Disclaimer - I dont think this is meant to be fun, but rather educational. It does a good job of guiding you linearly through the process of performing CPR on a person in the park. I found at moments I was more focused on finding where to line up my hands with the green ghost hands in the scenario, and at times accidentally triggering the next steps. It’s a clean and straightforward experience, and good as a beginner tutorial. I would like to see a more challenging mode that doesn’t have those green ghost hands, and rather lets you pick up items and interact naturally. I would also love to see this expanded to a home environment and how to do CPR on a child (most parents like me have probably worried about if they could do CPR on their child). One minor confusion, there was no explanation about the AED box and what it is. I also had trouble taking the stickers off the electrodes. This is a great start, and I’m excited to see what will become of it. We need more educational experiences like this, it can literally save lives.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review, @GhwstVR! We're glad that you had a good learning experience in CPR Simulator.

We really appreciate that you've taken your time finding areas, where you believe CPR Simulator can be improved. 

We've noted that you would like to have expanded the CPR Simulator with the following functionality:

An option to remove the green ghost indicators. This is actually already possible if you create a custom scenario, and toggle 'Indicators' off.

The ability to train CPR on a child. This is actually something we're working on as I'm writing this message. We are in the finishing stages and hope to publish this new version with this option within the next 2 months.

I'm not sure exactly what you mean with 'missing explanation about the AED box'. Once you go to pick up the AED in the AED Box, the guide will tell you the details about the AED Box. If you would like to explain what you mean in more detail, please write us on feedback[at]aatevr.com.

Trouble taking the stickers off the electrodes. This has been noted. We have already done a few things to make it easy, but are of course always interested in making it even easier.

Thank you again for your feedback. Have a great day!

reviews.review_description: Covers the basics of CPR/AED and good feedback on the expected rhythm. Could help to have an option to skip rescue breaths mid-scenario, and hope to see children also covered in future as they do have a slightly different process.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review, @NotSoSly ! We're glad to hear that you had a good experience with CPR Simulator.

In our next update of CPR Simulator, coming out in 1-2 months, we're adding a toggle where you can turn rescue breaths off in the 'Customise Scenario' settings.

We're also planning to add options to train on children and infants in the future. If everything goes as we would like maybe already in Q2 2024.

We hope that when expansions are added to the CPR Simulator experience, that you would consider giving it a 5-star review.

Have a great day.

reviews.review_title: A great way to learn CPR
reviews.review_description: CPR Simulator is an innovative VR game that merges immersive technology with essential life-saving skills. Designed to teach proper CPR techniques, the game offers a realistic, hands-on approach that feels both engaging and educational. The detailed environments and responsive feedback systems allow users to practice chest compressions, breathing techniques, and emergency responses in a safe, controlled setting.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review, @MeiFagundes ! We're really happy that you can see a good potential in CPR Simulator, and that you enjoyed the interaction systems we developed for the scenario. Have a great day!

reviews.review_description:
I have learnt CPR at air cadets, and I can confirm this game is very realistic. The game included 99% of what I was taught, excluding to look at the chest for any respiratory movement while listening out for breaths.

The scenarios are realistic, and the game takes you through how to do CPR step by step and what to say as you call an ambulance .

It's much better and more realistic than online training videos.

I definitely feel more confident in doing CPR now.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review, @ballisticbond ! It means a lot to us. We're really glad that the learning experience resonated with you and your previous CPR training. It makes us proud to hear that you feel more confident doing CPR now. Have a great day.

reviews.review_description:
As someone who is CPR certified, this should count as a certification.

Everyone in the world should play this game so if the time comes when they need it they are ready.

Worth every penny.

developer_response.body: Really great to read your review. Thank you very much. We have also invested a lot of time and energy into CPR Simulator and will continue to do so, with the goal of making basic CPR training as available as possible. We're really glad you had a good experience with CPR Simulator. Have a great day.

reviews.review_description:
Please correct the last question ,right answer is recovery position without moving the patches.

Other scenarios ?Could you add more ?

developer_response.body:
Hej @StopHeartAttack! 

Thank you for your good review, and your feedback on the training scenario.

We understand that there might be different opinions about when or if to put the resuscitated person in recovery position. Because of this we have, since the release of CPR Simulator in 2020, consulted with a number of different first aid experts, that do first aid courses, whether what is the right thing to do in this situation.

According to the guidelines, if the resuscitated person is breathing normally, and seems responsive, there is no reason to use the recovery position.

We know that this choice will be based on the user's subjective feeling about what state the person is in, but we've tried to show that the person is breathing normally, have their eyes open, and seems responsive when the person is resuscitated.

That said, we've taken note that we should try to make it even more clear, that the resuscitated person is alive and in a habitual state, and therefore do not need to be put in a recovery position.

Thanks again for your review, and let us know if you have more feedback.

reviews.review_title: A nice simulator to keep your skills up
reviews.review_description:
Disclaimer: I received a free code to try this app.

CPR simulator does a very good job at explaining the process of attending to a person suffering a cardiac emergency.  Precise positioning is explained as well as the process from head tilt, to compressions, and even to AED usage.  You are performing against a timer and are responsible for summoning help, just as if it was a real world situation.  Victims are anatomically proper, with both male and female victim available for practice.  You are even given a guide you can “talk to” for assistance.  

This should not necessarily be used as a substitute for initial live in person training, but is an excellent refresher and practice tool to use after you receive certification.  As a law enforcement officer/first responder, I would highly recommend this app.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for your review, @NYRman1975!

We're very glad that you had a good experience with CPR Simulator, and look very much forward to expanding the first aid learning experience. 

Have a great day.

reviews.review_description:
Although some parts could be a bit more interactive, this is not a game, but a training tool. I was a first aid responder for ten years and have had more than twenty refresher courses. AED use was always a part of those trainings.

This tool will help you memorise the steps you need to take in situations of an unconscious victim (regardless of heart rhythm). It will also help with 'feeling the rhythm' of CPR.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your review, @Woutepout! We're glad that you can see a good learning potential in CPR Simulator. Soon you will be able to train CPR and the use of an AED on a child, which we look forward to releasing. Have a great day.

reviews.review_description: great idea to use consumer headsets to learn cpr as it is a life saver.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review, @Lord._.Green! It means a lot to us. Let us know what changes or additions it would take for you to give CPR Simulator 5 stars. We hope you will have a great day.

reviews.review_description:
As someone that has had to be CPR certified for work, I found this very useful and potentially a great tool for incorporation with in person trainings. The instructions and simulation were clear and informative. There are questions weaved into the scenario that make learning feel more experiential than just answering them in a workbook.

The way hand tracking is used to simulate chest compression is really well done. If you don't have a practice dummy this is a good was to actually measure the rate of the compressions. This is an app that I will continue to follow it's development because it is truly useful.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review, @MrAuk27! We're really happy that you have a had a good learning experience with CPR Simulator. We're also glad that you liked how the theory behind CPR and using an AED has been woven into the physical interactions. Have a great day!

reviews.review_title: Good for in case you forget the base
reviews.review_description: Really good for in case you forget or need to rewiew your skills !

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review, @lepfla. We're glad that you can see good educational value in the use of CPR Simulator. Have a great day!
